messages.content: rewrite: Hi, thank you for your expression of interest. We are currently actively seeking a qualified Early Year Educator (prefer native English speakers from USA, UK, CAN, AUS, IRE) for upcoming August semester. If you are interested in this opportunity, please feel free to reach out. Thank you very much!

Hello, we appreciate your interest. We are currently looking for a qualified Early Years Educator for the upcoming August semester. Preferably, we are seeking native English speakers from the USA, UK, Canada, Australia, or Ireland. If you are interested in this opportunity, please reach out. Thank you!

rewrite: Hi, thank you for your interest! We're sorry to inform you that for the upcoming August position, we are only considering native English speakers from USA, UK, CAN, AUS, IRE. We wish you the best of luck in your job search!

Hello, we appreciate your interest! Unfortunately, for the August position, we are only considering native English speakers from the USA, UK, Canada, Australia, or Ireland. We wish you the best of luck in your job search!

rewrite: Thank you for your interest in our school. After careful consideration, we regret to inform you that we are unable to meet your requirements at this stage, resulting in our decision to not proceed with your employment. We value your qualifications and skills and hope to have the opportunity to collaborate in the future. Wish you the best in your future endeavors.

Thank you for your interest in our school. After thorough consideration, we regret to inform you that we are currently unable to meet your requirements, leading us to decide not to proceed with your employment at this time. We appreciate your qualifications and skills and hope for the chance to collaborate in the future. Best wishes for your future endeavors.

rewrite: I have a Master’s degree in International Business Management, specializing in Human Resource Management from the University of Tasmania (QS Top 300). With 9 years of solid experience in HR-related fields across diverse industries, including international schools, consulting, finance, commerce, and tourism, I have gained extensive exposure to HR workflows and systems.

Fluent in English, Cantonese, and Mandarin, I excel in executing HR management tasks such as overseas recruitment, training, performance appraisal, and employee relations. I am committed to continuous learning, staying updated on human resource laws and regulations to effectively implement HR strategies in compliance with legal requirements.

Outgoing, detail-oriented, and with a strong sense of responsibility, I possess excellent communication and coordination skills, thriving in collaborative team environments. I am comfortable under pressure, adopting a problem-solving mindset to approach challenges with enthusiasm and determination.

I hold a Master’s degree in International Business Management, specializing in Human Resource Management from the University of Tasmania (QS Top 300). With 9 years of robust experience in various HR-related fields spanning international schools, consulting, finance, commerce, and tourism, I have acquired extensive knowledge of HR workflows and systems.

Proficient in English, Cantonese, and Mandarin, I excel in handling HR management responsibilities like overseas recruitment, training, performance appraisal, and employee relations. I am dedicated to ongoing learning, keeping abreast of HR laws and regulations to implement HR strategies in compliance with legal standards effectively.

Personable, detail-oriented, and possessed of a strong sense of accountability, I boast excellent communication and coordination abilities, thriving in collaborative team settings. I am adept at handling pressure, relying on a problem-solving approach to tackle challenges with eagerness and resolve.

When faced with pressure, I embrace a problem-solving mindset to overcome challenges with enthusiasm and determination.

rewrite: •	Streamlined staff management processes for positions such as school doctor, teaching assistant, and early years educator. Identified areas for improvement and implemented standardized procedures to enhance efficiency in these roles.
•	Successfully recruited and managed expat teachers at the Ersha and Huadu campuses. Provided comprehensive support to selected candidates, guiding them through the onboarding process and facilitating their integration into their new roles. Consistently achieved a recruitment rate of up to 5 candidates per month.
•	Managed the documentation process for work visas, work certificates, domestic and foreign work permissions, and other important documents for a team of 10 foreign teachers.
•	Facilitated seamless staff transfers, including overseas educators, to new roles. Assisted department heads in delivering performance feedback and conducting daily evaluations to motivate employees.
•	Collaborated with department heads to ensure effective communication between foreign teachers and other staff members. Facilitated the smooth transfer of information to meet the needs of key stakeholders within the school.
•	Develop a tailored background check process to collect & verify new hires' personal info, education, work experience. Contact former employers for feedback. Prepare detailed report for hiring decisions.
•	Regularly review organisational performance, improve performance management system, optimise annual process. Collect & summarise data for each department/position, maintain employee files.
•	Employee Engagement: Conduct regular interviews (2-3 times/semester) to understand employee needs, assist in care & training activities.

• Optimized staff management procedures for roles like school doctor, teaching assistant, and early years educator by identifying areas for enhancement and implementing standardized protocols to improve efficiency.
• Successfully recruited and oversaw expat teachers at Ersha and Huadu campuses, offering comprehensive support throughout the onboarding process and aiding in their successful integration into their new positions. Maintained a recruitment rate of up to 5 candidates per month consistently.
• Managed documentation processes for work visas, work certificates, domestic and international work permits, and other essential paperwork for a team of 10 foreign teachers.
• Oversaw smooth staff transitions, including those of overseas educators, to new roles, providing support to department heads in delivering performance feedback and conducting regular evaluations to boost employee motivation.
• Collaborated with department heads to ensure effective communication between foreign teachers and other staff members, facilitating the seamless transfer of information to meet the needs of stakeholders within the school.
• Developed a customized background check process to gather and verify new hires' personal information, educational qualifications, and work experience, including contacting previous employers for feedback and preparing comprehensive reports for hiring decisions.
• Regularly reviewed organizational performance to enhance the performance management system and streamline annual processes. Gathered and summarized data for each department/position while maintaining employee records.
• Employee Engagement: Conducted regular interviews (2-3 times per semester) to gauge employee needs, offering assistance in care and training activities.

I conducted regular interviews (2-3 times per semester) to assess employee needs and provide support in care and training activities.

rewrite: •	Developed a customized background check process to gather and verify new hires’ personal information, educational qualifications, and work experience, including contacting previous employers for feedback and preparing comprehensive reports for hiring decisions.

• Created a tailored background check procedure to collect and validate personal information, educational credentials, and work history of new hires. This process involved reaching out to former employers for references and compiling detailed reports to inform hiring decisions.

rewrite: 
•	Responsible for overseeing human resource operations for a workforce of approximately 3,000 full-time employees and 500 interns.
•	Actively participate in annual campus recruitment events at esteemed universities, including South China University of Technology and Guangdong University of Technology. Coordinate all recruitment activities, from scheduling meetings to conducting interviews, resulting in the acquisition of around 100 interns per year.
•	Manage onboarding, resignation, and position change procedures for both local and international employees, as well as interns. Regularly review employee files to ensure data accuracy and handle employment procedures, including visa applications for foreign employees.
•	Design and implement comprehensive onboarding training programs for new employees and interns. Develop training plans and courses to facilitate knowledge transfer of employee manuals, human resources systems, and processes. Conduct over 10 annual training sessions, engaging approximately 400 individuals.
•	Oversee staff attendance and conduct thorough audits of overtime applications. Regularly analyze attendance and overtime data, providing support to the Compensation and Benefits team for accurate salary calculations.
•	Compile and analyse data related to recruitment, onboarding, and resignation processes. Generate detailed recruitment analysis reports and HR data reports, offering valuable insights for future HR planning.
•	Support the GM of the South China Region in organizing global business meetings and annual conferences for account managers in Northeast Asia. Act as an interpreter, facilitating communication with foreign guests and stakeholders from headquarters.
•	Spearhead the establishment and modification of HR-related systems and processes. Streamline key processes such as resignation, onboarding, destination surveys, and training management, resulting in increased operational efficiency.

• Oversaw human resource operations for a staff of approximately 3,000 full-time employees and 500 interns.
• Actively participated in annual campus recruitment events at prestigious universities, including South China University of Technology and Guangdong University of Technology. Coordinated all recruitment activities, from scheduling meetings to conducting interviews, resulting in the recruitment of around 100 interns annually.
• Managed onboarding, resignation, and position change procedures for local and international employees, as well as interns. Regularly reviewed employee files, ensured data accuracy, and handled employment procedures, including visa applications for foreign employees.
• Designed and implemented comprehensive onboarding training programs for new employees and interns. Developed training plans and courses to facilitate the transfer of knowledge regarding employee manuals, human resources systems, and processes. Conducted over 10 annual training sessions engaging approximately 400 individuals.
• Oversaw staff attendance and conducted detailed audits of overtime applications. Regularly analyzed attendance and overtime data, providing support to the Compensation and Benefits team for accurate salary calculations.
• Compiled and analyzed data related to recruitment, onboarding, and resignation processes. Generated detailed recruitment analysis reports and HR data reports, offering valuable insights for future HR planning.
• Supported the GM of the South China Region in organizing global business meetings and annual conferences for account managers in Northeast Asia. Acted as an interpreter, facilitating communication with foreign guests and stakeholders from headquarters.
• Led the establishment and modification of HR-related systems and processes. Streamlined key processes such as resignation, onboarding, destination surveys, and training management to enhance operational efficiency.

rewrite: Project Description: Implemented Microsoft Power Platform, SharePoint, and Form integration to enhance the efficiency of the resignation procedure, replacing the time-consuming and manual system. Implemented automated reminders via email for employees who submit resignations.

Project Responsibilities: Led the optimization, processing, verification, and online operation of the project.

Project Achievement: Successfully automated the resignation process through active promotion. The system now sends a reminder email to employees on the day they submit their resignation, and a second email one day before their departure date, only if they have provided the necessary information in a timely manner. This resulted in a 50% reduction in operating time. Implemented across all Guangzhou branches, including Shunde Branch, Zhongshan Representative Office, Fangchenggang Branch, and Liuzhou Branch.

Project Description: Utilized Microsoft Power Platform, SharePoint, and Form integration to streamline the resignation process, replacing manual procedures with an automated system. Implemented email reminders for employees upon submission of resignations.

Project Responsibilities: Oversaw the optimization, validation, processing, and online implementation of the project.

Project Achievement: Successfully automated the resignation process through proactive implementation. The system now sends reminder emails to employees on the day of resignation submission and a follow-up email one day prior to their departure, provided all necessary information is submitted on time. This led to a 50% reduction in operational time. Implemented across all Guangzhou branches, including Shunde, Zhongshan Representative Office, Fangchenggang, and Liuzhou.

rewrite: Moring Kate, I hope you have great time with your family during Chinese New Year of Loong. We wish to updated you with our current recruitment of homeroom teacher (expat).

We are looking to finalise our decision in the coming March (no later than 4th March), and hopefully we will provide you with first drafted contract to you, and we could discuss from there.

Keep in touch, and let me know your thoughts anytime whenever you are free. Have a great day!

Good morning Kate, I hope you had a wonderful time with your family during the Chinese New Year of the Dragon. We would like to inform you about our ongoing recruitment process for the homeroom teacher position (expatriate).

We aim to reach a decision by the end of March (no later than the 4th), and we plan to provide you with the initial draft of the contract for your review. We can then discuss any further details from there.

Please stay in touch, and feel free to share your thoughts whenever you have a moment. Have a fantastic day!

rewirte: Hi Jones，

We appreciate your interest! Unfortunately, for the August position, we are only considering native English speakers from the USA, UK, Canada, Australia, Ireland or Scotland. 

If not, I am afraid we might not be able to proceed your application further at the current stage.

Thanks for your expression of interest again and have a great day!

Hello Jones,

Thank you for your interest! Regrettably, for the August position, we are specifically seeking native English speakers from the USA, UK, Canada, Australia, Ireland, or Scotland.

If this criteria does not align with your background, we may be unable to progress your application at this time.

We appreciate your interest once again and wish you a wonderful day!
rewrite: I pretty enjoy my time in Richmond, particulaly in the sunny warm spring, and cool sunny autumn
I thoroughly enjoy my time in Richmond, especially during the sunny and warm spring, and the cool and sunny autumn.

rewrite: Hi to all my connections, I am activily looking for new job opportunities in HR positions, which mainly focused on global recruitment, global people partner business, global HR operations, should your company or networks are looking for candidates like me, pleasea feel free to drop a message. Great thanks!

Hello to all my connections! I am actively seeking new job opportunities in HR roles, with a focus on global recruitment, global people partner business, and global HR operations. If your company or network is seeking candidates like me, please do not hesitate to reach out. Thank you!
